name: calendar
title: Calendar
description: A styled, accessible month grid built on react-day-picker. Renders a role=grid month table with roving tabindex, full arrow-key navigation (RTL-aware), aria-selected days, and a localized aria-label per day. Commons adds token styling, 44px day targets, an inline navigation chevron, and a non-color selected state. Supports single, multiple, and range selection modes with min/max/disabled dates.
useWhen: Browsing or choosing a date visually from a month grid
Selecting a start and end date (range mode) or several dates (multiple mode)
Building a date picker or availability view where the calendar itself is embedded
avoidWhen: Collecting a date the user already knows by heart such as a date of birth (use a memorable date — typing month/day/year is faster and more accessible)
Anchoring the calendar to a trigger input (use the date picker, which composes this calendar inside a popover)
Choosing from a small fixed set of dates (use a select or radio group)

keyboard: Tab moves to the previous/next month buttons, then into the grid at a single tab-focusable day (roving tabindex)
Left/Right Arrow move by one day and flip direction under dir=rtl; Up/Down Arrow move by one week
Home and End move to the first and last day of the focused week
PageUp and PageDown move to the same day in the previous/next month; add Shift to move by a year
Enter or Space selects the focused day
The month grid is role=grid; each day is a role=gridcell with aria-selected and a localized aria-label; the month caption is a polite live region announcing month changes
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each day button is 2.75rem square)
